title: Achnatherum pinetorum
intro: Achnatherum pinetorum, also known as pine drop grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Achnatherum pinetorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Achnatherum pinetorum is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
taxonomy: Achnatherum pinetorum is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Achnatherum, which is a group of perennial grasses. Achnatherum pinetorum is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae, a large and diverse group within the Poaceae family. The subfamily Pooideae is characterized by its grasses that have a two-flowered spikelet and a beaked glume. This classification places Achnatherum pinetorum among other grasses that are native to North America.
